# Particular

> Browser particle engine with Canvas 2D + WebGL2 rendering, React hooks and vanilla JS APIs.
> Zero-config defaults: WebGL renderer, auto-created canvas, auto-applied styles.

## Install

npm install particular

## Quick Start

### Vanilla — zero config
```js
import { createParticles } from "particular";
const p = createParticles();            // auto-creates canvas, appends to body, WebGL
p.attachClickBurst(document);           // click anywhere for particles
// p.destroy() to clean up
```

### Vanilla — text to particles
```js
import { createParticles } from "particular";
const p = createParticles({ preset: "imageText", mouseForce: true });
p.textToParticles("Hello");             // auto-centered, 1:1 size (capped at 50% viewport)
```

### Vanilla — image to particles
```js
import { createParticles } from "particular";
const p = createParticles({ preset: "imageShape", mouseForce: { strength: 3, radius: 80 } });
p.imageToParticles({ image: "/photo.png", width: 500 });
```

### Vanilla — screensaver
```js
import { startScreensaver } from "particular";
startScreensaver({ preset: "snow" });   // auto-creates canvas, continuous snowfall
```

### Vanilla — container mode
```js
import { createParticles } from "particular";
const p = createParticles({ container: document.getElementById("hero"), preset: "magic" });
p.attachClickBurst(document.getElementById("hero"));
// container must have position: relative
```

### React — click burst
```tsx
import { useParticles } from "particular";
function App() {
  const { canvasRef, canvasStyle, burstFromEvent } = useParticles();
  return (
    <>
      <canvas ref={canvasRef} style={canvasStyle} />
      <button onClick={burstFromEvent}>Burst</button>
    </>
  );
}
```

### React — screensaver
```tsx
import { useScreensaver } from "particular";
function Snow() {
  const { canvasRef, canvasStyle } = useScreensaver({ preset: "snow" });
  return <canvas ref={canvasRef} style={canvasStyle} />;
}
```

## API Reference

### createParticles(options?) → ParticlesController

Main entry point. Creates engine, canvas, and returns controller.

Options (all optional):
- canvas: HTMLCanvasElement          — if omitted, auto-created and appended to container or document.body
- preset: PresetName                 — default "magic". One of: "magic", "confetti", "fireworks", "fireworksDetonation", "snow", "meteors", "fireworksShow", "river", "imageText", "imageShape", "images"
- config: Partial<FullParticularConfig> — override any particle/engine config field
- renderer: "webgl" | "canvas"      — default "webgl"
- autoResize: boolean                — default true
- autoClick: boolean                 — default false. Auto-attach click burst
- clickTarget: EventTarget           — click target for autoClick
- container: HTMLElement             — container-aware mode. Canvas sizes to element, coords become relative
- mouseForce: boolean | MouseForceConfig — true = add tracking mouse force with defaults. Object = custom config

### ParticlesController

Returned by createParticles(). Methods:

#### Emission
- burst({ x, y, ...config? }) → Emitter           — emit particles at screen coordinates
- attachClickBurst(target?, overrides?) → cleanup   — auto-burst on click. Returns unsubscribe function

#### Forces
- addAttractor({ x, y, strength?, radius? }) → Attractor
- removeAttractor(attractor) → void
- addRandomAttractors(count, config?) → Attractor[]
- removeAllAttractors() → void
- addMouseForce(config?) → MouseForce               — config: { track?, strength?, radius?, damping?, maxSpeed?, falloff? }
- removeMouseForce(mouseForce) → void
- addBoundary({ element, strength?, radius?, inset? }) → BoundaryHandle — repulsion boundary around DOM element
- addContainerGlow(config) → ContainerGlowHandle                     — glowing particle halo around DOM element. See ContainerGlowConfig
- addMouseTrail(config?) → MouseTrailHandle                          — particle trail following cursor. See MouseTrailConfig

#### Effects
- explode(options?) → void                          — explode all particles into child fragments
- scatter({ velocity?, rotation? }?) → void           — random impulse. Particles with home positions spring back. rotation = rotational impulse (degrees/tick)
- startWobble(config?: WobbleConfig) → void           — start continuous per-frame nudges on all spring-return particles. When `track` element is provided, particles react to mouse proximity and velocity (directional push outward from image center, weighted by distance to cursor). Without `track`, falls back to simple random wobble. Suppresses settle-snap while active
- stopWobble() → void                                 — stop wobble and let particles settle back to home positions

#### Image/Text/Element
- imageToParticles(config) → Promise<Emitter>       — see ImageParticlesConfig below
- textToParticles(text, config?) → Promise<Emitter> — config is optional, auto-centers with smart defaults
- elementToParticles(element, config?) → Promise<Emitter> — capture any HTML element and replace with particles. See ElementParticlesConfig
- shatterImage(config) → Promise<Emitter>           — shatter an image into irregular polygon chunks that explode outward like broken glass. See ImageShatterConfig. Pass homeConfig for interactive mode (scatter/reassemble)
- shatterText(text, config?) → Promise<Emitter>    — render text to canvas and shatter into polygon chunks. config = ImageShatterConfig (minus image) + textConfig
- setIdleEffect(enabled) → void                    — toggle idle animations (breathing, wiggle, wave, pulse) on all home-position particles. Spring return still works when disabled

#### Properties
- engine: Particular                                 — the underlying engine instance
- canvas: HTMLCanvasElement                          — the canvas element (may have been auto-created)

#### Lifecycle
- destroy() → void                                  — clean up all resources, listeners, and auto-created canvas

### startScreensaver(options?) → ScreensaverController

Continuous ambient particle emission across viewport/container.

Options (all optional):
- canvas: HTMLCanvasElement          — if omitted, auto-created
- preset: PresetName                 — default "snow"
- config: Partial<FullParticularConfig>
- renderer: "webgl" | "canvas"      — default "webgl"
- autoResize: boolean                — default true
- mouseWind: MouseForceConfig | false — gentle mouse wind. false = disable
- container: HTMLElement

Returns: { engine, controller, destroy() }

### useParticles(options?) → UseParticlesResult (React hook)

Options (all optional):
- preset: PresetName                 — default "magic"
- config: Partial<FullParticularConfig>
- renderer: "webgl" | "canvas"      — default "webgl"
- autoResize: boolean                — default true
- autoClick: boolean                 — default false
- clickTarget: EventTarget
- backgroundLayer: boolean           — default true. Provides canvasStyle for full-viewport overlay
- container: HTMLElement
- mouseForce: boolean | MouseForceConfig

Returns:
- canvasRef: Ref<HTMLCanvasElement>   — attach to <canvas ref={canvasRef}>
- canvasStyle: CSSProperties | undefined — attach to <canvas style={canvasStyle}>
- controller: ParticlesController | null — full controller after mount
- burst(options) → void
- burstFromEvent(event, overrides?) → void — pass directly as onClick handler
- explode(options?) → void
- scatter(options?) → void
- startWobble(config?) → void
- stopWobble() → void
- imageToParticles(config) → void
- textToParticles(text, config?) → void
- elementToParticles(element, config?) → void
- shatterImage(config) → void
- shatterText(text, config?) → void
- setIdleEffect(enabled) → void

### useScreensaver(options?) → UseScreensaverResult (React hook)

Options (all optional):
- preset: PresetName                 — default "snow"
- config: Partial<FullParticularConfig>
- renderer: "webgl" | "canvas"      — default "webgl"
- autoResize: boolean                — default true
- backgroundLayer: boolean           — default true
- mouseWind: MouseForceConfig | false
- container: HTMLElement

Returns: { canvasRef, canvasStyle, destroy() }

## Config Types

### ImageParticlesConfig
- image: string | HTMLImageElement   — REQUIRED. URL, data URI, or element
- x?: number                        — default: center of viewport/container
- y?: number                        — default: center of viewport/container
- width?: number                    — default: image's natural width (1:1), capped at 50% viewport. Calculated from height if omitted
- height?: number                   — calculated from width + aspect ratio if omitted
- resolution?: number               — particles along longest axis. Default: 400 (square), 200 (circle/triangle)
- alphaThreshold?: number           — skip transparent pixels. Default 0.1
- particleSize?: number             — auto-calculated from grid if omitted
- particleLife?: number             — default Infinity (truly permanent — no lifetime countdown)
- gravity?: number                  — default 0
- fadeTime?: number                 — default 40
- scaleStep?: number                — default: instant (equal to size)
- shape?: ParticleShape             — default from preset
- homeConfig?: HomePositionConfig   — spring physics + idle animation config
- intro?: IntroConfig              — animated particle reveal. See IntroConfig
- autoCenter?: boolean             — re-create particles on resize to stay centered and scaled. Default true

### ElementParticlesConfig
Extends ImageParticlesConfig (minus `image`). Position and size auto-derived from element's bounding rect.
- hideElement?: boolean             — hide the original element after capture. Default true
- restoreElement?: boolean          — restore element visibility on destroy(). Default true
- All ImageParticlesConfig fields except `image` (x, y, width, height, resolution, shape, intro, homeConfig, etc.)

### ImageShatterConfig
- image: string | HTMLImageElement   — REQUIRED. URL, data URI, or element
- x?: number                        — default: center of viewport/container
- y?: number                        — default: center of viewport/container
- width?: number                    — default: image's natural width (1:1), capped at 50% viewport. Calculated from height if omitted
- height?: number                   — calculated from width + aspect ratio if omitted
- chunkCount?: number               — approximate number of chunks (actual = cols x rows). Default 36
- jitter?: number                   — grid jitter (0–1). Higher = more irregular shapes. Default 0.4
- velocity?: number                 — outward explosion velocity. Default 5
- velocitySpread?: number           — velocity randomness (0–1). Each chunk gets velocity x (1 +/- spread). Default 0.5
- gravity?: number                  — downward gravity on chunks. Default 0.12
- rotationSpeed?: number            — rotation speed (degrees/tick). Default 5
- particleLife?: number             — individual chunk lifetime in ticks. Default 120
- fadeTime?: number                 — fade out duration in ticks. Default 40
- friction?: number                 — friction applied to chunks. Default 0.005
- scaleStep?: number                — how quickly chunks grow to full size. Default 100 (instant)
- homeConfig?: HomePositionConfig   — when provided, enables interactive mode: chunks get spring-return home positions. Use scatter() for impulse or startWobble()/stopWobble() for continuous organic motion. Gravity and fadeTime are ignored in this mode

### IntroConfig (for image/text particles)
- mode?: "scatter" | "scaleIn" | "ripple" | "paint" — animation mode. Default "scatter"
  - "scatter": particles start at random positions, spring to home while scaling in
  - "scaleIn": particles fly outward from image center — outer edges form first, filling inward to center
  - "ripple": shockwave from center — particles appear and get pushed outward, overshoot, spring back
  - "paint": particles spray from bottom center, staggered left-to-right, painting the image
- duration?: number                — total intro duration in ms. Default 800

### MouseForceConfig
- strength?: number                 — default 1
- radius?: number                   — default 50
- damping?: number                  — velocity decay per frame. Default 0.85
- maxSpeed?: number                 — default 10
- falloff?: number                  — <1 = broad wind, 1 = linear (default), >1 = localized push
- track?: EventTarget | boolean     — true = track mouse/touch on window. Element = track on element. Supports both mouse and touch input

### AttractorConfig
- x: number                        — REQUIRED. Engine coordinates
- y: number                        — REQUIRED. Engine coordinates
- strength?: number                 — default 1. Negative = repulsion
- radius?: number                   — default 150
- visible?: boolean                 — render the attractor. Default false
- icon?: string | HTMLImageElement
- size?: number                     — visual size. Default 12
- color?: string                    — default "#adb5bd"
- shape?: ParticleShape

### BoundaryConfig
- element: HTMLElement              — REQUIRED. DOM element to create boundary around
- strength?: number                 — default -1.5 (repulsion)
- radius?: number                   — repulsion distance from edge. Default 10
- inset?: number                    — 0-1 fraction. Default 0.4

### ContainerGlowConfig
- element: HTMLElement              — REQUIRED. DOM element to glow around
- colors?: string[]                 — default: soft blue-to-purple palette
- rate?: number                     — emission rate per edge. Default 0.5
- sizeMin?: number                  — default 0.5
- sizeMax?: number                  — default 2
- particleLife?: number             — default 60
- fadeTime?: number                 — default 30
- velocity?: number                 — outward velocity. Default 0.4
- spread?: number                   — spread angle in radians. Default 0.3
- friction?: number                 — default 0.01
- shape?: ParticleShape             — default "sparkle"
- glow?: boolean                    — default true
- glowColor?: string                — default "#74c0fc"
- glowAlpha?: number                — default 0.35
- glowSize?: number                 — default 8
- blendMode?: BlendMode             — default "additive"
- pulseSpeed?: number               — sinusoidal pulse speed. 0 = off. Default 0.02
- pulseAmplitude?: number           — pulse amplitude (0–1). Default 0.4

ContainerGlowHandle: { update(), stop(), start(), destroy() }
- stop() — stop emitting, existing particles fade naturally
- start() — resume emitting after stop()
- update() — rebuild emitter positions (called automatically on resize)

### MouseTrailConfig
- target?: EventTarget               — element to track mouse on. Default: window
- colors?: string[]                  — default: soft blue-to-purple palette
- rate?: number                      — emission rate while moving. Default 1.5
- sizeMin?: number                   — default 1
- sizeMax?: number                   — default 3
- particleLife?: number              — default 40
- fadeTime?: number                  — default 20
- velocity?: number                  — base velocity magnitude. Default 1.5
- spread?: number                    — spread angle in radians. Default 0.8
- friction?: number                  — default 0.02
- shape?: ParticleShape              — default "sparkle"
- glow?: boolean                     — default true
- glowColor?: string                 — default "#74c0fc"
- glowAlpha?: number                 — default 0.4
- glowSize?: number                  — default 10
- blendMode?: BlendMode              — default "additive"
- trail?: boolean                    — enable particle streak. Default true
- trailLength?: number               — default 6
- trailFade?: number                 — default 0.4
- trailShrink?: number               — default 0.5
- minSpeed?: number                  — min mouse speed to emit. Default 0.5

MouseTrailHandle: { stop(), start(), destroy() }
- stop() — pause emission, existing particles fade naturally
- start() — resume after stop()

### WobbleConfig
- velocity?: number                  — base outward velocity per frame. Default 0.8
- rotation?: number                  — rotational jitter per frame. Default 0.4
- track?: HTMLElement                — element to track mouse/touch on. Enables mouse-reactive directional wobble: particles near cursor get pushed harder outward from image center, mouse velocity drags nearby particles. Without this, falls back to simple random wobble (backward compatible)
- mouseRadius?: number               — radius of mouse influence in screen px. Default 200
- mouseStrength?: number             — push multiplier for particles near mouse. Default 3

### HomePositionConfig (for image/text particles)
- springStrength?: number           — default 0.05
- springDamping?: number            — default 0.9
- homeThreshold?: number            — idle engage distance. Default 2
- velocityThreshold?: number        — idle engage speed. Default 0.5
- wiggleAmplitude?: number          — random scale pulsing (0-1). Default 0
- wiggleSpeed?: number              — wiggle animation speed. Default 0.05
- breathingAmplitude?: number       — sinusoidal scale oscillation (0-1). Default 0
- breathingSpeed?: number           — breathing animation speed. Default 0.03
- waveAmplitude?: number            — coordinated scale wave (0-1). Default 0
- waveSpeed?: number                — wave propagation speed. Default 0.03
- waveFrequency?: number            — wave spatial frequency. Default 0.15
- returnNoise?: number              — wobble during spring return. Default 0.3
- idlePulseStrength?: number        — periodic impulse magnitude. Default 2
- idlePulseIntervalMin?: number     — min ticks between pulses. Default 300
- idlePulseIntervalMax?: number     — max ticks between pulses. Default 1800

### Engine Config Fields (ParticularConfig)
- maxCount: number                   — max alive particles. Default 500
- pixelRatio: number                 — canvas pixel ratio. Default 2
- continuous: boolean                — keep emitters alive after burst. Default false
- particlePoolSize: number           — max dead particles kept in reuse pool. Default 2000. Higher = less GC, more idle memory
- webglMaxInstances: number          — WebGL max particles per draw call. Default 4096
- container: HTMLElement             — container-aware mode element

### setParticlePoolSize(size: number): void
Set the max dead particles kept in the reuse pool at runtime. Also exported as a standalone function.
Pool auto-shrinks if new limit is smaller than current pool size. Default 2000.

### Key Particle Config Fields
- shape: "circle" | "rectangle" | "square" | "roundedRectangle" | "triangle" | "star" | "ring" | "sparkle"
- blendMode: "normal" | "additive" | "multiply" | "screen"
- rate: number                      — particles per frame. Default 8
- life: number                      — emitter burst budget. Default 30
- particleLife: number              — particle lifetime in ticks. Default 100. Use Infinity for permanent particles (no lifetime countdown). Particles with homePosition are automatically permanent
- velocity: Vector                  — initial velocity direction + speed
- spread: number                    — emission cone width in radians
- sizeMin/sizeMax: number           — particle size range
- velocityMultiplier: number        — each particle's speed = velocity × random(0, multiplier). Higher = more variation. Default 6
- fadeTime: number                  — fade-out duration in ticks. Default 30
- gravity: number                   — downward pull. Default 0.15
- gravityJitter: number             — per-particle gravity randomness (0–1). Each particle's gravity is multiplied by a random factor in the range `1 ± gravityJitter`, giving heavier and lighter particles even at the same size. Default 0
- colors: string[]                  — hex color palette. Empty = auto-generated harmonious palette
- trail: boolean                    — enable trail rendering
- trailLength: number               — max trail segments
- glow: boolean                     — enable glow effect
- glowSize: number                  — glow radius
- shadow: boolean                   — enable drop shadow
- detonate: { at: number, childCount?: number, velocity?: number, ... } — auto-explode at lifetime fraction

## Presets

| Name                  | Type    | Description                                          |
|-----------------------|---------|------------------------------------------------------|
| "magic"               | Burst   | Glowing sparkles with soft trails, additive blending (default) |
| "confetti"            | Burst   | Colorful rectangle confetti, muted colors            |
| "fireworks"           | Burst   | Glowing triangles with trailing streaks, additive    |
| "fireworksDetonation" | Burst   | Trailing triangle rockets, auto-explode into sub-bursts |
| "snow"                | Ambient | Gentle snowfall, continuous, gravity jitter for natural drift |
| "meteors"             | Ambient | Bright diagonal streaks with glowing trails          |
| "fireworksShow"       | Ambient | Continuous fireworks — triangle rockets launch and detonate |
| "river"               | Ambient | Horizontal water stream for use with attractors      |
| "imageText"           | Image   | Tiny square particles for text rendering             |
| "imageShape"          | Image   | Circle particles with glow for image/icon rendering  |
| "images"              | Image   | Icon/image burst particles                           |

## Color Palettes

Override colors on any preset: `createParticles({ config: { colors: ["#ff0000", "#00ff00"] } })`

Built-in (via `presets.Colors.xxx`): snow, grayscale, coolBlue, muted, blue, orange, green, meteor, water, finland, usa.
Empty colors array = auto-generated harmonious HSL palette per emitter.

## Container Mode

Pass `container: HTMLElement` to any function. The container must have `position: relative`.
Canvas auto-sizes to container. All coordinates become container-relative.
Works with `addBoundary()` for DOM element repulsion inside the container.

## Common Patterns

### Click burst on specific element
```js
const p = createParticles({ container: myElement });
p.attachClickBurst(myElement);
```

### Mouse trail (magic wisps following cursor)
```js
const p = createParticles({ config: { continuous: true } });
p.addMouseTrail(); // default sparkle wisps with trails
// p.addMouseTrail({ colors: ["#ff9500", "#ffb347"], trail: false }) for custom look
```

### Interactive text with mouse push
```js
const p = createParticles({ preset: "imageText", mouseForce: { strength: 3, radius: 80 } });
p.textToParticles("Hello");
```

### Particles that avoid DOM elements
```js
const p = createParticles({ container: pageContainer, preset: "snow" });
const handle = p.addBoundary({ element: document.querySelector(".card"), strength: -4, radius: 35 });
// handle.destroy() to remove
```

### Fireworks with auto-detonation
```js
const p = createParticles({ preset: "fireworksDetonation" });
p.attachClickBurst(document);
```

### Image with intro animation
```js
const p = createParticles({ preset: "imageShape", mouseForce: true });
p.imageToParticles({ image: "/photo.png", width: 500, intro: {} }); // scatter (default)
p.imageToParticles({ image: "/photo.png", width: 500, intro: { mode: "ripple" } }); // center-out wave
```

### Dissolve an HTML element into particles
```js
const p = createParticles({ container: document.getElementById("app"), preset: "imageShape", mouseForce: true });
const heading = document.querySelector("h1");
p.elementToParticles(heading, { intro: { mode: "ripple" } });
// Element is hidden, particles appear in its place. Restored on p.destroy().
```

### Shatter an image into glass-like pieces
```js
const p = createParticles({ config: { continuous: true } });
p.shatterImage({ image: "/photo.png", width: 500 });
// Fine pieces: p.shatterImage({ image: "/photo.png", chunkCount: 120, velocity: 8, gravity: 0.15 });
```

### Interactive shatter (hover to break, leave to reassemble)
```js
const p = createParticles({ container, config: { continuous: true } });
await p.shatterImage({
  image: "/photo.png",
  homeConfig: { springStrength: 0.06, springDamping: 0.85 },
});
container.addEventListener("mouseenter", () => {
  p.scatter({ velocity: 5, rotation: 3 });
  p.startWobble({ track: container });
});
container.addEventListener("mouseleave", () => p.stopWobble());
// Mouse-reactive wobble: particles near cursor get pushed harder. stopWobble lets spring system pull chunks back
```

### Shatter text (interactive)
```js
const p = createParticles({ container, config: { continuous: true } });
await p.shatterText("HELLO", {
  chunkCount: 64,
  homeConfig: { springStrength: 0.06, springDamping: 0.85 },
  textConfig: { fontSize: 180, fontWeight: "bold" },
});
container.addEventListener("mouseenter", () => {
  p.scatter({ velocity: 5, rotation: 3 });
  p.startWobble({ track: container });
});
container.addEventListener("mouseleave", () => p.stopWobble());
```

### Container glow around an element
```js
const p = createParticles({ container: myContainer, config: { continuous: true } });
const glow = p.addContainerGlow({ element: document.querySelector(".card") });
// glow.stop() / glow.start() for hover interactions
// glow.destroy() to remove
```

### Container glow on text (hover)
```js
const p = createParticles({ container, config: { continuous: true } });
const glow = p.addContainerGlow({ element: heading, colors: ["#ff9500", "#ffb347"] });
glow.stop(); // start paused
heading.addEventListener("mouseenter", () => glow.start());
heading.addEventListener("mouseleave", () => glow.stop());
```

### Custom colors
```js
const p = createParticles({ config: { colors: ["#ff6b6b", "#feca57", "#48dbfb"] } });
```

## Notes

- All positions in controller methods use screen pixels (CSS pixels). Engine divides by pixelRatio internally.
- Attractor positions use engine coordinates (screen / pixelRatio). Use addAttractor for direct control.
- Image/text particles have spring return — they bounce back to home positions after being pushed.
- shadow is disabled by default (`shadow: false`). Only the confetti preset enables it for visual depth. Enable with `config: { shadow: true }` when needed — it uses extra GPU resources.
- destroy() cleans up everything: canvas (if auto-created), event listeners, resize observers, RAF loop. Child handles (BoundaryHandle, ContainerGlowHandle, MouseTrailHandle) are also cleaned up by controller.destroy(). Calling handle.destroy() after controller.destroy() is safe (idempotent).
- Container elements must have `position: relative` (or absolute/fixed). A warning is logged if position is static.
- createParticles() requires a browser environment. In SSR/Node.js, wrap the call in useEffect or guard with `typeof document !== 'undefined'`.
- TypeScript types are included. All config interfaces are exported.
